headline: Today's AI engineering radar — 2026-06-26
summary: Today's lead story: "Where Larger Models Excel: The Primacy of Constraint-Guided Reasoning" (arXiv cs.CL). Coverage spans infra_tooling, papers_benchmarks, robotics_vla with 449 items scanned and 5 surfaced across sections.

items.title: Where Larger Models Excel: The Primacy of Constraint-Guided Reasoning
items.summary: arXiv:2606.26108v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Larger language models consistently outperform smaller ones on reasoning benchmarks, yet the reasoning differences underlying this gap remain underexplored. Across benchmarks in mathematics, physics, chemistry, and programming, we observe stable performance gaps: averaged over datasets, Qwen3-32B outperforms Qwen3-8B by 6.43%, while GPT-OSS-120B exceeds GPT-OSS-20B by 7.38%. To study the reasoning differences behind these gains, we develop AdvClus…

items.title: A System for Fast, Resilient, and Adaptable Loco-Manipulation Behaviors on Humanoid Robots
items.summary: arXiv:2606.26425v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Humanoid robots could take on physically demanding, hazardous, and repetitive work in spaces built for humans. However, a useful robot for these spaces must coordinate locomotion, whole body motion, perception, contact, and operator supervision. This thesis presents a robot-local, runtime-editable behavior authoring and runtime system. Our system strives to be maximally observable, predictable, and directable following Coactive Design principles d…
